name: woocommerce-code-review
description: Review WooCommerce code changes for coding standards compliance. Use when reviewing code locally, performing automated PR reviews, or checking code quality.
capabilities: Detects PHP architecture violations like standalone functions and incorrect dependency injection
Identifies naming convention violations including camelCase and Yoda conditions
Flags missing documentation requirements like @since annotations
Checks UI text for proper sentence case usage
Provides specific fixes with links to relevant skill documentation
author: woocommerce
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill providing coding standards guidelines. Contains no executable code, network calls, or file system operations. Static findings are false positives from markdown link syntax and JSON text being misidentified as security patterns.
valueStatement: Ensuring code quality in WooCommerce development can be challenging with multiple coding standards. This skill automates code review by checking against WooCommerce's specific PHP, JavaScript, and UI text guidelines.
limitations: Only reviews code against WooCommerce-specific standards
Requires manual invocation for each code review
Limited to the standards documented in related WooCommerce skills

useCases.title: Ensure Code Quality
useCases.description: Review pull requests for compliance with WooCommerce coding standards before merging

useCases.title: Automated Standards Check
useCases.description: Quickly identify critical violations in PHP and JavaScript code changes

useCases.title: Maintain Consistency
useCases.description: Standardize code across team members following WooCommerce conventions

promptTemplates.title: Basic PHP Review
promptTemplates.scenario: Review PHP code for standards compliance
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this WooCommerce PHP code for critical violations: [paste code]. Check for standalone functions, incorrect DI usage, naming conventions, and missing documentation.

promptTemplates.title: UI Text Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Check these WooCommerce UI strings for proper sentence case: [paste text]. Flag any Title Case usage that should be sentence case.

promptTemplates.title: Architecture Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this WooCommerce code for dependency injection violations. Check if classes use $container->get() instead of new, and if internal classes are in src/Internal/.

promptTemplates.title: Complete PR Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Perform a complete WooCommerce code review on this PR. Check PHP standards, UI text, documentation, and architecture. Group violations by type with specific fixes.

bestPractices: Always cite specific skill documentation when flagging violations
Group related issues together for clearer feedback
Provide correct examples from skill documentation in your review
antiPatterns: Don't flag style preferences not covered in WooCommerce standards
Avoid reviewing code without citing the relevant skill documentation
Never assume standards - always reference specific skill files

faq.question: What coding standards does this skill check?
faq.answer: It checks WooCommerce-specific standards for PHP architecture, naming conventions, documentation, and UI text formatting.

faq.question: Can this skill automatically fix code violations?
faq.answer: No, it only identifies violations and provides guidance on how to fix them with examples from skill documentation.

faq.question: How do I use this with pull request reviews?
faq.answer: Invoke the skill when reviewing PRs to automatically check code against WooCommerce standards before approval.

faq.question: Does it check JavaScript/React code too?
faq.answer: Yes, it checks UI text in JavaScript/React code for proper sentence case usage according to WooCommerce guidelines.

faq.question: What if the standards conflict with my preferences?
faq.answer: Follow WooCommerce standards when contributing to WooCommerce core. These are project-specific requirements.

faq.question: How detailed are the violation reports?
faq.answer: Reports include violation type, exact location, link to relevant skill documentation, and specific fix guidance.
